transcript.speaker: paramedic
transcript.text: Ma'am, I'm Alex with county EMS. Can you tell me what happened?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: I've had chest pain for about two hours. It started suddenly while I was gardening.

transcript.speaker: paramedic
transcript.text: Do you have any allergies to medications? Any current medications or medical history?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: I'm allergic to penicillin — I get a rash. I take lisinopril for my blood pressure.

transcript.speaker: paramedic
transcript.text: Any blood thinners or heart medications?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Yes, warfarin. I had a heart valve replacement three years ago.

transcript.speaker: paramedic
transcript.text: I found a medication bag at the scene. Let me check what's in here before we arrive.

timeline.summary: Scene Arrival — Residential address, garden setting
Patient reports acute chest pain, 2hr duration, exertional onset
⚠ Anticoagulation + chest pain — ACS workup indicated
En Route — County General, ETA 8 minutes
⚠ Vision scan: aspirin 325mg — patient is on warfarin (bleeding risk)
Hospital Arrival — County General ED bay 3

safetyFlags.concern: Warfarin + chest pain — anticoagulation complicates ACS management
safetyFlags.rationale: Patient on warfarin presenting with chest pain and left arm radiation. Balance bleeding vs thrombotic risk.

safetyFlags.concern: Vision scan: aspirin 325mg — patient is on warfarin (bleeding risk)
safetyFlags.rationale: Vial scan identified 'aspirin 325mg' on scene. Patient has documented warfarin therapy. Concurrent aspirin + warfarin significantly increases GI and intracranial bleeding risk. Verify before administration.

safetyFlags.concern: NREMT gap: Last oral intake not yet documented (SAMPLE history)
safetyFlags.rationale: NREMT primary assessment: Last oral intake not yet documented (SAMPLE history). Ensure this is addressed before transport.

soap.subjective: 67F with 2hr acute chest pain, exertional onset, left arm radiation, mild SOB. PMH: HTN, mechanical heart valve. Meds: lisinopril, warfarin. Allergy: penicillin.
soap.objective: BP 158/94, HR 92, SpO2 96% on room air → 98% on 4L NC. 12-lead ECG: sinus rhythm. Aspirin 325mg vial identified on scene via camera.
soap.assessment: Acute chest pain concerning for ACS in anticoagulated patient with mechanical valve and cardiac history.
soap.plan: Supplemental O2, IV access, continuous monitoring. Transport to County General. ED: ECG, troponin, weigh bleeding vs thrombotic risk before antiplatelet. Withhold aspirin pending physician review (warfarin interaction).

research.findings: Warfarin-treated patients presenting with ACS require careful balance of antithrombotic therapy. INR monitoring is critical before initiating additional antiplatelet agents.

citations.title: 2023 ACC/AHA Guideline for Diagnosis and Management of Acute Coronary Syndromes
citations.snippet: For patients on oral anticoagulation presenting with ACS, individualized decision-making is required for antiplatelet co-therapy.

citations.title: Warfarin Drug Interactions — FDA Prescribing Information
citations.snippet: Increased bleeding risk with concurrent antiplatelet agents. Monitor INR closely when aspirin is co-administered.

handoff.patientSummary: 67-year-old female presenting with 2-hour acute chest pain (exertional onset, left arm radiation, mild SOB). PMH: hypertension, mechanical heart valve. Medications: lisinopril, warfarin. Camera identified an aspirin 325mg vial on scene.
outstandingQuestions: Has the patient taken aspirin today?
recommendedActions: Stat ECG — review for ST changes
Check INR before initiating antiplatelet therapy — weigh bleed vs thrombosis risk
